projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The rapid drop in Wi-Fi devices (20 to 11) between 08:11 and 08:26, combined with zero camera motion, confirms the family is departing via the garage or an unmonitored exit, which validates the primary departure route for future automation triggers.

The stable count of 19–20 devices overnight with zero camera activity indicates the network is likely counting IoT/smart home hubs as mobile devices, and the household remains dormant; combined with Roxanne’s severe sleep deprivation, this confirms a quiet pre-morning state with no active movement or security concerns.
Roxanne slept only 2.89h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.
Pattern: late-night camera activity (after 10pm) on 3 of the last 7 days in locations: backyard, dog_run, entryway.

The sustained 15–19 connected devices paired with zero motion or camera activity over four hours indicates a false-positive presence pattern, likely caused by smart devices left on or connected while the household is asleep or stationary. This suggests presence detection should be cross-referenced with motion/activity to avoid triggering unnecessary automations.

The steady climb in connected devices (14→19) alongside zero motion/camera activity into the evening strongly suggests background IoT network activity or sensor drift rather than actual occupancy, warranting a quick check to rule out offline motion detectors or false presence readings.

The consistent 14–17 connected devices paired with zero motion/camera on a Monday afternoon, combined with Roxanne’s documented 2.6-hour sleep deficit, indicates the household is home but resting or working quietly rather than being away. This clarifies the repeated flags as a normal quiet day at home, not a security or connectivity anomaly.

The persistent high device count (15–17) paired with zero motion/camera activity for four hours, combined with Roxanne’s severe sleep deprivation, suggests the household is in a prolonged low-energy rest state rather than being away. However, given the Australian Shepherds’ high activity levels, this also warrants a quick sensor verification to rule out a motion detection outage.

The persistent 15–19 device count paired with zero motion/camera activity on a weekday afternoon strongly indicates a false positive in presence detection, likely from IoT devices or static network connections being miscounted as mobile phones. This matters because occupancy-based automations (security, climate, or alerts) may be incorrectly triggered or suppressed.
Roxanne slept only 2.61h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.
